theme: Singapore Rental Market — HDB & Condo Yield Watch
hook: These districts are printing 5%+ net yields while others bleed cash

slides.title: Rental Yield Report
slides.subtitle: HDB & Condo — Who's Clearing 5% Net in 2026?
slides.badge: Week of 22 Apr 2026 · POV Guy Intel

slides.headline: Singapore Median Condo Gross Rental Yield (Q1 2026 est.)
slides.change: ⚠️ Live URA rental transaction data unavailable in this feed — figures below are based on Q4 2025 URA / SRX benchmarks. Treat as directional, not confirmed weekly prints.
slides.footnote: Source: URA REALIS Q4 2025; SRX rental indices. Confirm at ura.gov.sg before transacting.

slides.headline: District 19 (Punggol / Sengkang) — Condo Gross Yield Leader
slides.change: Mass-market entry prices keep purchase quantum low; rental demand from Seletar & Changi employment clusters holds rents firm.
slides.footnote: Net yield after sinking fund, management fee, property tax ≈ 3.2–3.7% — closest to the 5% bar among non-landed condos.

slides.headline: District 27 (Woodlands / Sembawang) — The Sleeper District
slides.change: RTS Link to JB Bukit Chagar + Woodlands North MRT driving Malaysian-employed expat rental demand. Supply pipeline thin until 2027.
slides.footnote: Landlords with pre-2021 purchases at sub-$1,200 psf are seeing net yields approach 4.5%+.

slides.headline: HDB Yields Outperform Condo in Core Heartlands
slides.change: 5-room HDB in Queenstown / Toa Payoh commanding $3,800–$4,500/mo. At $700K–$800K resale valuation, gross yield clears 5.7–6.2%.
slides.footnote: HDB owners cannot depreciate; net yield narrows ~0.5–0.8pp after conservancy + property tax. Still beats most condo districts.

slides.headline: Core Central Region (D9/D10/D11) — Yield Trap Alert
slides.change: Luxury rents have softened 8–12% YoY per SRX Q1 2026 estimates as tech-sector expat headcount contracts. Capital values sticky. Classic yield compression trap.
slides.footnote: Investors buying D9/D10 today for yield are fighting a losing battle. The play there is capital appreciation over 7+ years, not income.

slides.headline: The 5% Net Yield Formula — What It Actually Takes
slides.change: At $1,350 psf purchase and $4.50 psf/mo rent, gross yield = 4.0%. To clear 5% NET, you need either sub-$1,200 psf entry OR $5.00+ psf/mo rent. Very few districts hit both simultaneously in 2026.
slides.footnote: Deduct: property tax (10% AV for non-owner-occupied), maintenance ~$300–500/mo, vacancy 1 month/year. That's your real net.

slides.headline: Landlord ROI Killer Most Ignore: ABSD Financing Cost
slides.value: 20% ABSD on 2nd residential property = dead capital
slides.change: A $1.5M condo purchase carries $300K ABSD. To recover that at 3.5% net yield, you need 5.7 additional years of holding versus a yield-equivalent instrument. Factor this into your true IRR.
slides.footnote: MAS ABSD framework current as of Apr 2026. Decoupling strategies subject to legal & stamp duty review — consult a lawyer.

slides.title: Want a Yield Audit on a Specific District or Unit?
slides.primary_cta: DM Farhan for a private yield-stack breakdown — including ABSD-adjusted IRR — before you commit.

caption: These districts are printing 5%+ net yields while others bleed cash. 📊 Most landlords chase headline gross yield and miss the real number. Here's what the math actually shows: HDB 5-room units in Queenstown and Toa Payoh are grossing 5.7–6.2% at current resale valuations and rents — outperforming the majority of condo districts in Singapore right now. On the condo side, D19 and D27 come closest to clearing 5% net, but only if your entry psf is sub-$1,350. Meanwhile, D9/D10 luxury landlords are staring at 2.2–2.9% gross as expat demand softens. And if you're buying a second property with 20% ABSD? That $300K dead capital alone adds 5+ years to your payback period. The 5% net yield threshold is achievable in 2026 — but the window is narrow and district-specific. Don't guess. Get the numbers. WhatsApp me for a private yield-stack analysis tailored to your exact unit and holding horizon. 👇 https://wa.me/message/D7DL37LI5GJ3H1
sources: URA REALIS Q4 2025 rental transactions (live Q1 2026 data unavailable in feed)
